channel.title: Single Galley on Kitchen Remodel Ideas
channel.description: Recent content in Single Galley on Kitchen Remodel Ideas

item.title: New Hampton Island Floor Plan
item.author: chris@kitchenremodelideas.com (Kitchen Remodel Ideas)
item.description: Kitchen Shape: Single Galley Flooring: Laminate Flooring (Click Together Flooring) Subfloor: Wood Insulation Surface: 156 sq. ft. Voltage: 120 V Installation Options: TempZone™ 3.7-Watt Floor Heating Cable: “The WarmlyYours TempZone™ 3.7-Watt Floor Heating Cable allows you to bring radiant warmth to any room. TempZone™ Floor Heating Cable (Twin) is ultra-low EMF by design.

item.title: Single Galley One Wall Kitchen
item.author: chris@kitchenremodelideas.com (Kitchen Remodel Ideas)
item.description: Kitchen Shape: Single Galley Flooring: Resilients, Vinyl and Luxury Vinyl Tile (LVT) Subfloor: Existing Concrete Slab Insulation Surface: 149 sq. ft. Voltage: 240 V Installation Options: Tempzone Floor Heating Cable: “The WarmlyYours TempZone™ 3.7-Watt Floor Heating Cable allows you to bring radiant warmth to any room. TempZone™ Floor Heating Cable (Twin) is ultra-low EMF by design.

item.title: Single Galley California Kitchen
item.author: chris@kitchenremodelideas.com (Kitchen Remodel Ideas)
item.description: Kitchen Shape: Single Galley Flooring: Tile, Marble, or Stone Subfloor: Wood Insulation Surface: 129 sq. ft. Voltage: 120 V Installation Options: Tempzone Flex Roll: “Add radiant warmth to your tile and stone floors with WarmlyYours TempZone™ Flex Rolls. These 240 VAC floor heating rolls feature a 1/8” TempZone™ heating cable, affixed to a mesh fabric backing in a serpentine pattern to evenly distribute heat across the floor.

item.title: First Floor Single Galley Kitchen
item.author: chris@kitchenremodelideas.com (Kitchen Remodel Ideas)
item.description: Kitchen Shape: Single Galley Flooring: Tile, Marble, or Stone Subfloor: Wood Insulation Surface: 201 sq. ft. Voltage: 240 V Installation Options: Tempzone Flex Rolls: “Hard flooring surfaces can be cold underfoot any time of year, but are especially so when the weather gets cooler. Who wouldn’t enjoy the comfort of a WarmlyYours radiant in-floor heating system in their home?
